17: # along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
18: # Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
19: 
20: class Changeset < ApplicationRecord
21:   belongs_to :repository
22:   belongs_to :user
23:   has_many :filechanges, :class_name => 'Change', :dependent => :delete_all
24:   has_and_belongs_to_many :issues
25:   has_and_belongs_to_many :parents,
26:                           :class_name => "Changeset",
27:                           :join_table => "#{table_name_prefix}changeset_parents#{table_name_suffix}",
28:                           :association_foreign_key => 'parent_id', :foreign_key => 'changeset_id'
29:   has_and_belongs_to_many :children,
30:                           :class_name => "Changeset",
31:                           :join_table => "#{table_name_prefix}changeset_parents#{table_name_suffix}",
32:                           :association_foreign_key => 'changeset_id', :foreign_key => 'parent_id'
33: 
34:   acts_as_event(
35:     :title => proc {|o| o.title},
36:     :description => :long_comments,
37:     :datetime => :committed_on,
38:     :url =>
39:       proc do |o|
40:         {:controller => 'repositories', :action => 'revision',
41:          :id => o.repository.project,
42:          :repository_id => o.repository.identifier_param, :rev => o.identifier}
43:       end
44:   )
45:   acts_as_searchable :columns => 'comments',
46:                      :preload => {:repository => :project},
47:                      :project_key => "#{Repository.table_name}.project_id",
48:                      :date_column => :committed_on
49: 
50:   acts_as_activity_provider :timestamp => "#{table_name}.committed_on",
51:                             :author_key => :user_id,
52:                             :scope => proc {preload(:user, {:repository => :project})}
53: 
54:   validates_presence_of :repository_id, :revision, :committed_on, :commit_date
55:   validates_uniqueness_of :revision, :scope => :repository_id, :case_sensitive => true
56:   validates_uniqueness_of :scmid, :scope => :repository_id, :allow_nil => true, :case_sensitive => true
57: 
58:   scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
59:     joins(:repository => :project).
60:     where(Project.allowed_to_condition(args.shift || User.current, :view_changesets, *args))
61:   end)
62: 
63:   before_create :before_create_cs
64:   after_create :scan_for_issues
65: 
66:   def revision=(r)
67:     write_attribute :revision, (r.nil? ? nil : r.to_s)
68:   end
69: 
70:   # Returns the identifier of this changeset; depending on repository backends
71:   def identifier
72:     if repository.class.respond_to? :changeset_identifier
73:       repository.class.changeset_identifier self
74:     else
75:       revision.to_s
76:     end
77:   end
78: 
79:   def committed_on=(date)
80:     self.commit_date = date
81:     super
82:   end
83: 
84:   # Returns the readable identifier
85:   def format_identifier
86:     if repository.class.respond_to? :format_changeset_identifier
87:       repository.class.format_changeset_identifier self
88:     else
89:       identifier
90:     end
91:   end
92: 
93:   def project
94:     repository.project
95:   end
96: 
97:   def author
98:     user || committer.to_s.split('<').first
99:   end
100: 
101:   def before_create_cs
102:     self.committer = self.class.to_utf8(self.committer, repository.repo_log_encoding)
103:     self.comments =
104:       self.class.normalize_comments(self.comments, repository.repo_log_encoding)
105:     self.user = repository.find_committer_user(self.committer)
106:   end
107: 
108:   def scan_for_issues
109:     scan_comment_for_issue_ids
110:   end
111: 
112:   TIMELOG_RE = /
113:     (
114:     ((\d+)(h|hours?))((\d+)(m|min)?)?
115:     |
116:     ((\d+)(h|hours?|m|min))
117:     |
118:     (\d+):(\d+)
119:     |
120:     (\d+([.,]\d+)?)h?
121:     )
122:     /x
123: 
124:   def scan_comment_for_issue_ids
125:     return if comments.blank?
126: 
127:     # keywords used to reference issues
128:     ref_keywords = Setting.commit_ref_keywords.downcase.split(",").collect(&:strip)
129:     ref_keywords_any = ref_keywords.delete('*')
130: 
131:     # keywords used to fix issues
132:     fix_keywords = Setting.commit_update_keywords_array.pluck('keywords').flatten.compact
133:     kw_regexp = (ref_keywords + fix_keywords).collect{|kw| Regexp.escape(kw)}.join("|")
134: 
135:     referenced_issues = []
136:     regexp =
137:       %r{
138:         ([\s(\[,-]|^)((#{kw_regexp})[\s:]+)?
139:         (\#\d+(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?([\s,;&]+\#\d+(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?)*)
140:         (?=[[:punct:]]|\s|<|$)
141:       }xi
142:     comments.scan(regexp) do |match|
143:       action = match[2].to_s.downcase
144:       refs   = match[3]
145:       next unless action.present? || ref_keywords_any
146: 
147:       refs.scan(/#(\d+)(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?/o).each do |m|
148:         issue = find_referenced_issue_by_id(m[0].to_i)
149:         hours = m[2]
150:         if issue && !issue_linked_to_same_commit?(issue)
151:           referenced_issues << issue
152:           # Don't update issues or log time when importing old commits
153:           unless repository.created_on && committed_on && committed_on < repository.created_on
154:             fix_issue(issue, action) if fix_keywords.include?(action)
155:             log_time(issue, hours) if hours && Setting.commit_logtime_enabled?
156:           end
157:         end
158:       end
159:     end
160: 
161:     referenced_issues.uniq!
162:     self.issues = referenced_issues unless referenced_issues.empty?
163:   end
164: 
165:   def short_comments
166:     @short_comments || split_comments.first
167:   end
168: 
169:   def long_comments
170:     @long_comments || split_comments.last
171:   end
172: 
173:   def text_tag(ref_project=nil)
174:     repo = ""
175:     if repository && repository.identifier.present?
176:       repo = "#{repository.identifier}|"
177:     end
178:     tag = scmid? ? "commit:#{repo}#{scmid}" : "#{repo}r#{revision}"
179:     if ref_project && project && ref_project != project
180:       tag = "#{project.identifier}:#{tag}"
181:     end
182:     tag
183:   end
184: 
185:   # Returns the title used for the changeset in the activity/search results
186:   def title
187:     repo = (repository && repository.identifier.present?) ? " (#{repository.identifier})" : ''
188:     comm = short_comments.blank? ? '' : (': ' + short_comments)
189:     "#{l(:label_revision)} #{format_identifier}#{repo}#{comm}"
190:   end
191: 
192:   # Returns the previous changeset
193:   def previous
194:     @previous ||= repository&.previous_changeset(self)
195:   end
196: 
197:   # Returns the next changeset
198:   def next
199:     @next ||= repository&.next_changeset(self)
200:   end
201: 
202:   # Creates a new Change from it's common parameters
203:   def create_change(change)
204:     Change.create(:changeset     => self,
205:                   :action        => change[:action],
206:                   :path          => change[:path],
207:                   :from_path     => change[:from_path],
208:                   :from_revision => change[:from_revision])
209:   end
210: 
211:   # Finds an issue that can be referenced by the commit message
212:   def find_referenced_issue_by_id(id)
213:     return nil if id.blank?
214: 
215:     issue = Issue.find_by_id(id.to_i)
216:     if Setting.commit_cross_project_ref?
217:       # all issues can be referenced/fixed
218:     elsif issue
219:       # issue that belong to the repository project, a subproject or a parent project only
220:       unless issue.project &&
221:                 (project == issue.project || project.is_ancestor_of?(issue.project) ||
222:                  project.is_descendant_of?(issue.project))
223:         issue = nil
224:       end
225:     end
226:     issue
227:   end
228: 
229:   private
230: 
231:   # Returns true if the issue is already linked to the same commit
232:   # from a different repository
233:   def issue_linked_to_same_commit?(issue)
234:     repository.same_commits_in_scope(issue.changesets, self).any?
235:   end
236: 
237:   # Updates the +issue+ according to +action+
238:   def fix_issue(issue, action)
239:     # the issue may have been updated by the closure of another one (eg. duplicate)
240:     issue.reload
241:     # don't change the status is the issue is closed
242:     return if issue.closed?
243: 
244:     journal = issue.init_journal(user || User.anonymous,
245:                                  ll(Setting.default_language,
246:                                     :text_status_changed_by_changeset,
247:                                     text_tag(issue.project)))
248:     rule = Setting.commit_update_keywords_array.detect do |rule|
249:       rule['keywords'].include?(action) &&
250:         (rule['if_tracker_id'].blank? || rule['if_tracker_id'] == issue.tracker_id.to_s)
251:     end
252:     if rule
253:       issue.assign_attributes rule.slice(*Issue.attribute_names)
254:     end
255:     Redmine::Hook.call_hook(:model_changeset_scan_commit_for_issue_ids_pre_issue_update,
256:                             {:changeset => self, :issue => issue, :action => action})
257: 
258:     if issue.changes.any?
259:       unless issue.save
260:         logger.warn("Issue ##{issue.id} could not be saved by changeset #{id}: #{issue.errors.full_messages}") if logger
261:       end
262:     else
263:       issue.clear_journal
264:     end
265:     issue
266:   end
267: 
268:   def log_time(issue, hours)
269:     time_entry =
270:       TimeEntry.new(
271:         :user => user,
272:         :hours => hours,
273:         :issue => issue,
274:         :spent_on => commit_date,
275:         :comments => l(:text_time_logged_by_changeset, :value => text_tag(issue.project),
276:                        :locale => Setting.default_language)
277:       )
278:     if activity = issue.project.commit_logtime_activity
279:       time_entry.activity = activity
280:     end
281: 
282:     unless time_entry.save
283:       logger.warn("TimeEntry could not be created by changeset #{id}: #{time_entry.errors.full_messages}") if logger
284:     end
285:     time_entry
286:   end
287: 
288:   def split_comments
289:     comments =~ /\A(.+?)\r?\n(.*)$/m
290:     @short_comments = $1 || comments
291:     @long_comments = $2.to_s.strip
292:     [@short_comments, @long_comments]
293:   end
294: 
295:   # Singleton class method is public
296:   class << self
297:     # Strips and reencodes a commit log before insertion into the database
298:     def normalize_comments(str, encoding)
299:       Changeset.to_utf8(str.to_s, encoding).strip
300:     end
301: 
302:     def to_utf8(str, encoding)
303:       Redmine::CodesetUtil.to_utf8(str, encoding)
304:     end
305:   end
306: end

376:   # Updates the filename and description of a set of attachments
377:   # with the given hash of attributes. Returns true if all
378:   # attachments were updated.
379:   #
380:   # Example:
381:   #   Attachment.update_attachments(attachments, {
382:   #     4 => {:filename => 'foo'},
383:   #     7 => {:filename => 'bar', :description => 'file description'}
384:   #   })
385:   #
386:   def self.update_attachments(attachments, params)
387:     converted = {}
388:     params.each {|key, val| converted[key.to_i] = val}
389:     saved = true
390:     transaction do
391:       attachments.each do |attachment|
392:         if file = converted[attachment.id]
393:           attachment.filename = file[:filename] if file.key?(:filename)
394:           attachment.description = file[:description] if file.key?(:description)
395:           saved &&= attachment.save
396:         end
397:       end
398:       unless saved
399:         raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
400:       end
401:     end
402:     saved
403:   end

417:   def self.archive_attachments(attachments)
418:     attachments = attachments.select(&:readable?)
419:     return nil if attachments.blank?
420: 
421:     Zip.unicode_names = true
422:     archived_file_names = []
423:     buffer = Zip::OutputStream.write_buffer do |zos|
424:       attachments.each do |attachment|
425:         filename = attachment.filename
426:         # rename the file if a file with the same name already exists
427:         dup_count = 0
428:         while archived_file_names.include?(filename)
429:           dup_count += 1
430:           extname = File.extname(attachment.filename)
431:           basename = File.basename(attachment.filename, extname)
432:           filename = "#{basename}(#{dup_count})#{extname}"
433:         end
434:         zos.put_next_entry(filename)
435:         zos << IO.binread(attachment.diskfile)
436:         archived_file_names << filename
437:       end
438:     end
439:     buffer.string
440:   ensure
441:     buffer&.close
442:   end

455:       logger.error "Could not create directory #{File.dirname(dest)}" if logger
456:       return
457:     end
458: 
459:     unless FileUtils.mv(src, dest)
460:       logger.error "Could not move attachment from #{src} to #{dest}" if logger
461:       return

476:   # (ie. created before Redmine 3.4)

479:       attachment.update_digest_to_sha256!

483:   # Updates attachment digest to SHA256
484:   def update_digest_to_sha256!
485:     if readable?
486:       sha = Digest::SHA256.new
487:       File.open(diskfile, 'rb') do |f|
488:         while buffer = f.read(8192)
489:           sha.update(buffer)
490:         end
491:       end
492:       update_column :digest, sha.hexdigest
493:     end
494:   end

496:   # Returns true if the extension is allowed regarding allowed/denied
497:   # extensions defined in application settings, otherwise false
498:   def self.valid_extension?(extension)
499:     denied, allowed = [:attachment_extensions_denied, :attachment_extensions_allowed].map do |setting|
500:       Setting.send(setting)
501:     end
502:     if denied.present? && extension_in?(extension, denied)
503:       return false
504:     end
505:     if allowed.present? && !extension_in?(extension, allowed)
506:       return false
507:     end
508: 
509:     true
510:   end
511: 
512:   # Returns true if extension belongs to extensions list.
513:   def self.extension_in?(extension, extensions)
514:     extension = extension.downcase.sub(/\A\.+/, '')
515: 
516:     unless extensions.is_a?(Array)
517:       extensions = extensions.to_s.split(",").map(&:strip)
518:     end
519:     extensions = extensions.map {|s| s.downcase.sub(/\A\.+/, '')}.reject(&:blank?)
520:     extensions.include?(extension)
521:   end
522: 
523:   # Returns true if attachment's extension belongs to extensions list.
524:   def extension_in?(extensions)
525:     self.class.extension_in?(File.extname(filename), extensions)
526:   end
527: 
528:   # returns either MD5 or SHA256 depending on the way self.digest was computed
529:   def digest_type
530:     digest.size < 64 ? "MD5" : "SHA256" if digest.present?
531:   end
532: 
533:   private
534: 
535:   def reuse_existing_file_if_possible
536:     original_diskfile = diskfile
537:     original_filename = disk_filename
538:     reused = with_lock do
539:       if existing = Attachment
540:                       .where(digest: self.digest, filesize: self.filesize)
541:                       .where.not(disk_filename: original_filename)
542:                       .order(:id)
543:                       .last
544:         existing.with_lock do
545:           existing_diskfile = existing.diskfile
546:           if File.readable?(original_diskfile) &&
547:             File.readable?(existing_diskfile) &&
548:             FileUtils.identical?(original_diskfile, existing_diskfile)
549:             self.update_columns disk_directory: existing.disk_directory,
550:                                 disk_filename: existing.disk_filename
551:           end
552:         end
553:       end
554:     end
555:     if reused && Attachment.where(disk_filename: original_filename).none?
556:       File.delete(original_diskfile)
557:     end
558:   rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid, ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
559:     # Catch and ignore lock errors. It is not critical if deduplication does
560:     # not happen, therefore we do not retry.
561:     # with_lock throws ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound if the record isnt there
562:     # anymore, thats why this is caught and ignored as well.
563:   end
564: 
565:   # Physically deletes the file from the file system
566:   def delete_from_disk!
567:     FileUtils.rm_f(diskfile) if disk_filename.present?
568:     Dir[thumbnail_path("*")].each do |thumb|
569:       File.delete(thumb)
570:     end
571:     FileUtils.rm_f(markdownized_preview_cache_path)
572:   end
573: 
574:   def thumbnail_path(size)
575:     File.join(self.class.thumbnails_storage_path,
576:               "#{digest}_#{filesize}_#{size}.thumb")
577:   end
578: 
579:   def sanitize_filename(value)
580:     # get only the filename, not the whole path
581:     just_filename = value.gsub(/\A.*(\\|\/)/m, '')
582: 
583:     # Finally, replace invalid characters with underscore
584:     just_filename.gsub(/[\/?%*:|"'<>\n\r]+/, '_')
585:   end
586: 
587:   # Returns the subdirectory in which the attachment will be saved
588:   def target_directory
589:     time = created_on || DateTime.now
590:     time.strftime("%Y/%m")
591:   end
592: 
593:   # Singleton class method is public
594:   class << self
595:     # Claims a unique ASCII or hashed filename, yields the open file handle
596:     def create_diskfile(filename, directory=nil, &)
597:       timestamp = DateTime.now.strftime("%y%m%d%H%M%S")
598:       ascii = ''
599:       if %r{^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*$}.match?(filename) && filename.length <= 50
600:         ascii = filename
601:       else
602:         ascii = ActiveSupport::Digest.hexdigest(filename)
603:         # keep the extension if any
604:         ascii << $1 if filename =~ %r{(\.[a-zA-Z0-9]+)$}
605:       end
606: 
607:       path = File.join storage_path, directory.to_s
608:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(path) unless File.directory?(path)
609:       begin
610:         name = "#{timestamp}_#{ascii}"
611:         File.open(
612:           File.join(path, name),
613:           flags: File::CREAT | File::EXCL | File::WRONLY,
614:           binmode: true,
615:           &
616:         )
617:       rescue Errno::EEXIST
618:         timestamp.succ!
619:         retry
620:       end
621:     end
622:   end
623: end

19: 
20: class Watcher < ApplicationRecord
21:   belongs_to :watchable, :polymorphic => true
22:   belongs_to :user, :class_name => 'Principal'
23: 
24:   validates_presence_of :user
25:   validates_uniqueness_of :user_id, :scope => [:watchable_type, :watchable_id], :case_sensitive => true
26:   validate :validate_user
27: 
28:   # Returns true if at least one object among objects is watched by user
29:   def self.any_watched?(objects, user)
30:     objects = objects.reject(&:new_record?)
31:     if objects.any?
32:       objects.group_by {|object| object.class.base_class}.each do |base_class, objects|
33:         if Watcher.where(:watchable_type => base_class.name, :watchable_id => objects.map(&:id), :user_id => user.id).exists?
34:           return true
35:         end
36:       end
37:     end
38:     false
39:   end
40: 
41:   # Unwatch things that users are no longer allowed to view
42:   def self.prune(options={})
43:     if options.has_key?(:user)
44:       prune_single_user(options[:user], options)
45:     else
46:       pruned = 0
47:       User.where("id IN (SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM #{table_name})").each do |user|
48:         pruned += prune_single_user(user, options)
49:       end
50:       pruned
51:     end
52:   end
53: 
54:   protected
55: 
56:   def validate_user
57:     errors.add :user_id, :invalid \
58:       unless user.nil? || (user.is_a?(User) && user.active?) || (user.is_a?(Group) && user.givable?)
59:   end
60: 
61:   def self.prune_single_user(user, options={})
62:     return unless user.is_a?(User)
63: 
64:     pruned = 0
65:     where(:user_id => user.id).each do |watcher|
66:       next if watcher.watchable.nil?
67: 
68:       if options.has_key?(:project)
69:         unless watcher.watchable.respond_to?(:project) &&
70:                  watcher.watchable.project == options[:project]
71:           next
72:         end
73:       end
74:       if watcher.watchable.respond_to?(:visible?)
75:         unless watcher.watchable.visible?(user)
76:           watcher.destroy
77:           pruned += 1
78:         end
79:       end
80:     end
81:     pruned
82:   end
83:   private_class_method :prune_single_user
84: end

20: class Reaction < ApplicationRecord
21:   belongs_to :reactable, polymorphic: true
22:   belongs_to :user
23: 
24:   validates :reactable_type, inclusion: { in: Redmine::Reaction::REACTABLE_TYPES }
25: 
26:   scope :by, ->(user) { where(user: user) }
27:   scope :for_reactable, ->(reactable) { where(reactable: reactable) }
28:   scope :visible, ->(user) { where(user: User.visible(user)) }
29: 
30:   # Represents reaction details for a reactable object
31:   Detail = Struct.new(
32:     # Users who reacted and are visible to the target user
33:     :visible_users,
34:     # Reaction of the target user
35:     :user_reaction
36:   ) do
37:     def initialize(visible_users: [], user_reaction: nil)
38:       super
39:     end
40: 
41:     def reaction_count = visible_users.size
42:   end
43: 
44:   def self.build_detail_map_for(reactables, user)
45:     reactions = visible(user)
46:                   .for_reactable(reactables)
47:                   .preload(:user)
48:                   .select(:id, :reactable_id, :user_id)
49:                   .order(id: :desc)
50: 
51:     reactions.each_with_object({}) do |reaction, m|
52:       m[reaction.reactable_id] ||= Detail.new
53: 
54:       m[reaction.reactable_id].then do |detail|
55:         detail.visible_users << reaction.user
56:         detail.user_reaction = reaction if reaction.user == user
57:       end
58:     end
59:   end
60: end

298:       delete_available_filter field

302:   def available_columns
303:     return @available_columns if @available_columns
304: 
305:     @available_columns = self.class.available_columns.dup
306:     @available_columns += issue_custom_fields.visible.collect {|cf| QueryCustomFieldColumn.new(cf)}
307: 
308:     if User.current.allowed_to?(:view_time_entries, project, :global => true)
309:       # insert the columns after total_estimated_hours or at the end
310:       index = @available_columns.find_index {|column| column.name == :total_estimated_hours}
311:       index = (index ? index + 1 : -1)
312: 
313:       subselect = "SELECT SUM(hours) FROM #{TimeEntry.table_name}" +
314:         " JOIN #{Project.table_name} ON #{Project.table_name}.id = #{TimeEntry.table_name}.project_id" +
315:         " WHERE (#{TimeEntry.visible_condition(User.current)}) AND #{TimeEntry.table_name}.issue_id = #{Issue.table_name}.id"
316: 
317:       @available_columns.insert(
318:         index,
319:         QueryColumn.new(:spent_hours,
320:                         :sortable => "COALESCE((#{subselect}), 0)",
321:                         :default_order => 'desc',
322:                         :caption => :label_spent_time,
323:                         :totalable => true)
324:       )
325: 
326:       subselect = "SELECT SUM(hours) FROM #{TimeEntry.table_name}" +
327:         " JOIN #{Project.table_name} ON #{Project.table_name}.id = #{TimeEntry.table_name}.project_id" +
328:         " JOIN #{Issue.table_name} subtasks ON subtasks.id = #{TimeEntry.table_name}.issue_id" +
329:         " WHERE (#{TimeEntry.visible_condition(User.current)})" +
330:         " AND subtasks.root_id = #{Issue.table_name}.root_id AND subtasks.lft >= #{Issue.table_name}.lft AND subtasks.rgt <= #{Issue.table_name}.rgt"
331: 
332:       @available_columns.insert(
333:         index + 1,
334:         QueryColumn.new(:total_spent_hours,
335:                         :sortable => "COALESCE((#{subselect}), 0)",
336:                         :default_order => 'desc',
337:                         :caption => :label_total_spent_time)
338:       )
339:     end
340: 
341:     if User.current.allowed_to?(:set_issues_private, nil, :global => true) ||
342:       User.current.allowed_to?(:set_own_issues_private, nil, :global => true)
343:       @available_columns <<
344:         QueryColumn.new(:is_private,
345:                         :sortable => "#{Issue.table_name}.is_private", :groupable => true)
346:     end
347: 
348:     disabled_fields = Tracker.disabled_core_fields(trackers).map {|field| field.delete_suffix('_id')}
349:     if disabled_fields.include?("estimated_hours")
350:       disabled_fields += %w[total_estimated_hours estimated_remaining_hours]
351:     end
352:     @available_columns.reject! do |column|
353:       disabled_fields.include?(column.name.to_s)
354:     end
355: 
356:     @available_columns
357:   end

404:   # Returns the issues
405:   # Valid options are :order, :offset, :limit, :include, :conditions
406:   def issues(options={})
407:     order_option = [group_by_sort_order, (options[:order] || sort_clause)].flatten.reject(&:blank?)
408:     # The default order of IssueQuery is issues.id DESC(by IssueQuery#default_sort_criteria)
409:     unless ["#{Issue.table_name}.id ASC", "#{Issue.table_name}.id DESC"].any?{|i| order_option.include?(i)}
410:       order_option << "#{Issue.table_name}.id DESC"
411:     end
412: 
413:     scope = base_scope.
414:       preload(:priority).
415:       includes(([:status, :project] + (options[:include] || [])).uniq).
416:       where(options[:conditions]).
417:       order(order_option).
418:       joins(joins_for_order_statement(order_option.join(','))).
419:       limit(options[:limit]).
420:       offset(options[:offset])
421: 
422:     scope =
423:       scope.preload(
424:         [:tracker, :author, :assigned_to, :fixed_version,
425:          :category, :attachments] & columns.map(&:name)
426:       )
427:     if has_custom_field_column?
428:       scope = scope.preload(:custom_values)
429:     end
430:     if has_column?(:watcher_users)
431:       scope = scope.preload(:watcher_users)
432:     end
433: 
434:     issues = scope.to_a
435: 
436:     if has_column?(:spent_hours)
437:       Issue.load_visible_spent_hours(issues)
438:     end
439:     if has_column?(:total_spent_hours)
440:       Issue.load_visible_total_spent_hours(issues)
441:     end
442:     if has_column?(:last_updated_by)
443:       Issue.load_visible_last_updated_by(issues)
444:     end
445:     if has_column?(:relations)
446:       Issue.load_visible_relations(issues)
447:     end
448:     if has_column?(:last_notes)
449:       Issue.load_visible_last_notes(issues)
450:     end
451:     issues
452:   rescue ::ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
453:     raise StatementInvalid.new(e.message)
454:   end

505:   def sql_for_notes_field(field, operator, value)
506:     subquery = "SELECT 1 FROM #{Journal.table_name}" +
507:       " WHERE #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_type='Issue' AND #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_id=#{Issue.table_name}.id" +
508:       " AND (#{sql_for_field field, operator.delete_prefix('!'), value, Journal.table_name, 'notes'})" +
509:       " AND (#{Journal.visible_notes_condition(User.current, :skip_pre_condition => true)})"
510:     "#{operator.start_with?('!') ? "NOT EXISTS" : "EXISTS"} (#{subquery})"
511:   end
512: 
513:   def sql_for_updated_by_field(field, operator, value)
514:     neg = (operator == '!' ? 'NOT' : '')
515:     subquery = "SELECT 1 FROM #{Journal.table_name}" +
516:       " WHERE #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_type='Issue' AND #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_id=#{Issue.table_name}.id" +
517:       " AND (#{sql_for_field field, '=', value, Journal.table_name, 'user_id'})" +
518:       " AND (#{Journal.visible_notes_condition(User.current, :skip_pre_condition => true)})"
519: 
520:     "#{neg} EXISTS (#{subquery})"
521:   end
522: 
523:   def sql_for_last_updated_by_field(field, operator, value)
524:     neg = (operator == '!' ? 'NOT' : '')
525:     subquery = "SELECT 1 FROM #{Journal.table_name} sj" +
526:       " WHERE sj.journalized_type='Issue' AND sj.journalized_id=#{Issue.table_name}.id AND (#{sql_for_field field, '=', value, 'sj', 'user_id'})" +
527:       " AND sj.id IN (SELECT MAX(#{Journal.table_name}.id) FROM #{Journal.table_name}" +
528:       "   WHERE #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_type='Issue' AND #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_id=#{Issue.table_name}.id" +
529:       "   AND (#{Journal.visible_notes_condition(User.current, :skip_pre_condition => true)}))"
530: 
531:     "#{neg} EXISTS (#{subquery})"
532:   end

538:   def sql_for_spent_time_field(field, operator, value)
539:     first, second = value.first.to_s.to_hours, value.second.to_s.to_hours
540:     sql_op =
541:       case operator
542:       when "=", ">=", "<=" then  "#{operator} #{first}"
543:       when "><"            then  "BETWEEN #{first} AND #{second}"
544:       when "*"             then  "> 0"
545:       when "!*"            then  "= 0"
546:       else
547:         return nil
548:       end
549:     "COALESCE((" +
550:       "SELECT ROUND(CAST(SUM(hours) AS DECIMAL(30,3)), 2) " +
551:       "FROM #{TimeEntry.table_name} " +
552:       "WHERE issue_id = #{Issue.table_name}.id), 0) #{sql_op}"
553:   end

555:   def sql_for_watcher_id_field(field, operator, value)
556:     db_table = Watcher.table_name
557:     me_ids = [0, User.current.id]
558:     me_ids.concat(User.current.groups.pluck(:id))
559:     me, others = value.partition {|id| me_ids.include?(id.to_i)}
560:     sql =
561:       if others.any?
562:         "SELECT #{Issue.table_name}.id FROM #{Issue.table_name} " +
563:         "INNER JOIN #{db_table} ON #{Issue.table_name}.id = #{db_table}.watchable_id AND #{db_table}.watchable_type = 'Issue' " +
564:         "LEFT OUTER JOIN #{Project.table_name} ON #{Project.table_name}.id = #{Issue.table_name}.project_id " +
565:         "WHERE (" +
566:           sql_for_field(field, '=', me, db_table, 'user_id') +
567:         ') OR (' +
568:           Project.allowed_to_condition(User.current, :view_issue_watchers) +
569:           ' AND ' +
570:           sql_for_field(field, '=', others, db_table, 'user_id') +
571:         ')'
572:       else
573:         "SELECT #{db_table}.watchable_id FROM #{db_table} " +
574:         "WHERE #{db_table}.watchable_type='Issue' AND " +
575:         sql_for_field(field, '=', me, db_table, 'user_id')
576:       end
577:     "#{Issue.table_name}.id #{ operator == '=' ? 'IN' : 'NOT IN' } (#{sql})"
578:   end

580:   def sql_for_member_of_group_field(field, operator, value)
581:     if operator == '*' # Any group
582:       groups = Group.givable
583:       operator = '=' # Override the operator since we want to find by assigned_to
584:     elsif operator == "!*"
585:       groups = Group.givable
586:       operator = '!' # Override the operator since we want to find by assigned_to
587:     else
588:       groups = Group.where(:id => value).to_a
589:     end
590:     groups ||= []
591: 
592:     members_of_groups = groups.inject([]) do |user_ids, group|
593:       user_ids + group.user_ids + [group.id]
594:     end.uniq.compact.sort.collect(&:to_s)
595: 
596:     '(' + sql_for_field("assigned_to_id", operator, members_of_groups, Issue.table_name, "assigned_to_id", false) + ')'
597:   end

599:   def sql_for_assigned_to_role_field(field, operator, value)
600:     case operator
601:     when "*", "!*" # Member / Not member
602:       sw = operator == "!*" ? 'NOT' : ''
603:       nl = operator == "!*" ? "#{Issue.table_name}.assigned_to_id IS NULL OR" : ''
604: 
605:       subquery =
606:         "SELECT 1" +
607:         " FROM #{Member.table_name}" +
608:         " WHERE #{Issue.table_name}.project_id = #{Member.table_name}.project_id AND #{Member.table_name}.user_id = #{Issue.table_name}.assigned_to_id"
609:       "(#{nl} #{sw} EXISTS (#{subquery}))"
610:     when "=", "!"
611:       role_cond =
612:         if value.any?
613:           "#{MemberRole.table_name}.role_id IN (" + value.collect{|val| "'#{self.class.connection.quote_string(val)}'"}.join(",") + ")"
614:         else
615:           "1=0"
616:         end
617:       sw = operator == "!" ? 'NOT' : ''
618:       nl = operator == "!" ? "#{Issue.table_name}.assigned_to_id IS NULL OR" : ''
619:       subquery =
620:         "SELECT 1" +
621:         " FROM #{Member.table_name} inner join #{MemberRole.table_name} on members.id = member_roles.member_id" +
622:         " WHERE #{Issue.table_name}.project_id = #{Member.table_name}.project_id AND #{Member.table_name}.user_id = #{Issue.table_name}.assigned_to_id AND #{role_cond}"
623:       "(#{nl} #{sw} EXISTS (#{subquery}))"
624:     end
625:   end

627:   def sql_for_author_group_field(field, operator, value)
628:     groups = value.empty? ? Group.givable : Group.where(:id => value).to_a
629: 
630:     author_groups = groups.inject([]) do |user_ids, group|
631:       user_ids + group.user_ids + [group.id]
632:     end.uniq.compact.sort.collect(&:to_s)
633: 
634:     '(' + sql_for_field("author_id", operator, author_groups, Issue.table_name, "author_id", false) + ')'
635:   end

637:   def sql_for_author_role_field(field, operator, value)

641:       else
642:         "1=0"
643:       end
644:     sw = operator == "!" ? 'NOT' : ''
645:     nl = operator == "!" ? "#{Issue.table_name}.author_id IS NULL OR" : ''
646:     subquery =
647:       "SELECT 1" +
648:       " FROM #{Member.table_name} inner join #{MemberRole.table_name} on members.id = member_roles.member_id" +
649:       " WHERE #{Issue.table_name}.project_id = #{Member.table_name}.project_id AND #{Member.table_name}.user_id = #{Issue.table_name}.author_id AND #{role_cond}"
650:     "(#{nl} #{sw} EXISTS (#{subquery}))"

680:   def sql_for_attachment_field(field, operator, value)
681:     case operator
682:     when "*", "!*"
683:       e = (operator == "*" ? "EXISTS" : "NOT EXISTS")
684:       "#{e} (SELECT 1 FROM #{Attachment.table_name} a WHERE a.container_type = 'Issue' AND a.container_id = #{Issue.table_name}.id)"
685:     when "~", "!~", "*~"
686:       c = sql_contains("a.filename", value.first, :all_words => (operator != "*~"))
687:       e = (operator == "!~" ? "NOT EXISTS" : "EXISTS")
688:       "#{e} (SELECT 1 FROM #{Attachment.table_name} a WHERE a.container_type = 'Issue' AND a.container_id = #{Issue.table_name}.id AND (#{c}))"
689:     when "^", "$"
690:       c = sql_contains("a.filename", value.first, (operator == "^" ? :starts_with : :ends_with) => true)
691:       "EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM #{Attachment.table_name} a WHERE a.container_type = 'Issue' AND a.container_id = #{Issue.table_name}.id AND (#{c}))"
692:     end
693:   end
694: 
695:   def sql_for_attachment_description_field(field, operator, value)
696:     cond_description = "a.description IS NOT NULL AND a.description <> ''"
697:     c =
698:       case operator
699:       when '*', '!*'
700:         (operator == '*' ? cond_description : "NOT (#{cond_description})")
701:       when '~', '!~', '*~'
702:         (operator == '~' ? '' : "#{cond_description} AND ") +
703:         sql_contains('a.description', value.first, :match => (operator != '!~'), :all_words => (operator != '*~'))
704:       when '^', '$'
705:         sql_contains('a.description', value.first, (operator == '^' ? :starts_with : :ends_with) => true)
706:       else
707:         '1=0'
708:       end
709:     "EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM #{Attachment.table_name} a WHERE a.container_type = 'Issue' AND a.container_id = #{Issue.table_name}.id AND (#{c}))"
710:   end

712:   def sql_for_parent_id_field(field, operator, value)
713:     case operator
714:     when "="
715:       # accepts a comma separated list of ids
716:       ids = value.first.to_s.scan(/\d+/).map(&:to_i).uniq
717:       if ids.present?
718:         "#{Issue.table_name}.parent_id IN (#{ids.join(",")})"
719:       else
720:         "1=0"
721:       end
722:     when "~"
723:       ids = value.first.to_s.scan(/\d+/).map(&:to_i).uniq
724:       conditions = ids.filter_map do |id|
725:         root_id, lft, rgt = Issue.where(id: id).pick(:root_id, :lft, :rgt)
726:         if root_id && lft && rgt
727:           "(#{Issue.table_name}.root_id = #{root_id} AND #{Issue.table_name}.lft > #{lft} AND #{Issue.table_name}.rgt < #{rgt})"
728:         else
729:           nil
730:         end
731:       end
732: 
733:       if conditions.any?
734:         "(#{conditions.join(' OR ')})"
735:       else
736:         "1=0"
737:       end
738:     when "!*"
739:       "#{Issue.table_name}.parent_id IS NULL"
740:     when "*"
741:       "#{Issue.table_name}.parent_id IS NOT NULL"
742:     end
743:   end
744: 
745:   def sql_for_child_id_field(field, operator, value)
746:     case operator
747:     when "="
748:       # accepts a comma separated list of child ids
749:       child_ids = value.first.to_s.scan(/\d+/).map(&:to_i).uniq
750:       ids = Issue.where(:id => child_ids).pluck(:parent_id).compact.uniq
751:       if ids.present?
752:         "#{Issue.table_name}.id IN (#{ids.join(",")})"
753:       else
754:         "1=0"
755:       end
756:     when "~"
757:       root_id, lft, rgt = Issue.where(:id => value.first.to_i).pick(:root_id, :lft, :rgt)
758:       if root_id && lft && rgt
759:         "#{Issue.table_name}.root_id = #{root_id} AND #{Issue.table_name}.lft < #{lft} AND #{Issue.table_name}.rgt > #{rgt}"
760:       else
761:         "1=0"
762:       end
763:     when "!*"
764:       "#{Issue.table_name}.rgt - #{Issue.table_name}.lft = 1"
765:     when "*"
766:       "#{Issue.table_name}.rgt - #{Issue.table_name}.lft > 1"
767:     end
768:   end
769: 
770:   def sql_for_updated_on_field(field, operator, value)
771:     case operator
772:     when "!*"
773:       "#{Issue.table_name}.updated_on = #{Issue.table_name}.created_on"
774:     when "*"
775:       "#{Issue.table_name}.updated_on > #{Issue.table_name}.created_on"
776:     else
777:       sql_for_field("updated_on", operator, value, Issue.table_name, "updated_on")
778:     end
779:   end

781:   def sql_for_issue_id_field(field, operator, value)
782:     if operator == "="
783:       # accepts a comma separated list of ids
784:       ids = value.first.to_s.scan(/\d+/).map(&:to_i)
785:       if ids.present?
786:         "#{Issue.table_name}.id IN (#{ids.join(",")})"
787:       else
788:         "1=0"
789:       end
790:     else
791:       sql_for_field("id", operator, value, Issue.table_name, "id")
792:     end
793:   end

795:   def sql_for_relations(field, operator, value, options={})
796:     relation_options = IssueRelation::TYPES[field]
797:     return relation_options unless relation_options
798: 
799:     relation_type = field
800:     join_column, target_join_column = "issue_from_id", "issue_to_id"

805:     sql =
806:       case operator
807:       when "*", "!*"
808:         op = (operator == "*" ? 'IN' : 'NOT IN')
809:         "#{Issue.table_name}.id #{op}" \
810:          " (SELECT DISTINCT #{IssueRelation.table_name}.#{join_column}" \
811:            " FROM #{IssueRelation.table_name}" \

814:       when "=", "!"

827:       when "=p", "=!p", "!p"
828:         op = (operator == "!p" ? 'NOT IN' : 'IN')
829:         comp = (operator == "=!p" ? '<>' : '=')
830:         "#{Issue.table_name}.id #{op}" \

832:            " FROM #{IssueRelation.table_name}, #{Issue.table_name} relissues" \

836:              " AND relissues.project_id #{comp} #{value.first.to_i})"
837:       when "*o", "!o"

845:              " AND relissues.status_id IN" \

20: module Redmine
21:   module SafeAttributes
22:     def self.included(base)
23:       base.extend(ClassMethods)
24:     end
25: 
26:     module ClassMethods
27:       # Declares safe attributes
28:       # An optional Proc can be given for conditional inclusion
29:       #
30:       # Example:
31:       #   safe_attributes 'title', 'pages'
32:       #   safe_attributes 'isbn', :if => {|book, user| book.author == user}
33:       def safe_attributes(*args)
34:         @safe_attributes ||= []
35:         if args.empty?
36:           if superclass.include?(Redmine::SafeAttributes)
37:             @safe_attributes + superclass.safe_attributes
38:           else
39:             @safe_attributes
40:           end
41:         else
42:           options = args.last.is_a?(Hash) ? args.pop : {}
43:           @safe_attributes << [args, options]
44:         end
45:       end
46:     end
47: 
48:     # Returns an array that can be safely set by user or current user
49:     #
50:     # Example:
51:     #   book.safe_attributes # => ['title', 'pages']
52:     #   book.safe_attributes(book.author) # => ['title', 'pages', 'isbn']
53:     def safe_attribute_names(user=nil)
54:       return @safe_attribute_names if @safe_attribute_names && user.nil?
55:       names = []
56:       self.class.safe_attributes.collect do |attrs, options|
57:         if options[:if].nil? || options[:if].call(self, user || User.current)
58:           names += attrs.collect(&:to_s)
59:         end
60:       end
61:       names.uniq!
62:       @safe_attribute_names = names if user.nil?
63:       names
64:     end
65: 
66:     # Returns true if attr can be set by user or the current user
67:     def safe_attribute?(attr, user=nil)
68:       safe_attribute_names(user).include?(attr.to_s)
69:     end
70: 
71:     # Returns a hash with unsafe attributes removed
72:     # from the given attrs hash
73:     #
74:     # Example:
75:     #   book.delete_unsafe_attributes({'title' => 'My book', 'foo' => 'bar'})
76:     #   # => {'title' => 'My book'}
77:     def delete_unsafe_attributes(attrs, user=User.current)
78:       safe = safe_attribute_names(user)
79:       attrs.dup.delete_if {|k, v| !safe.include?(k.to_s)}
80:     end
81: 
82:     # Sets attributes from attrs that are safe
83:     # attrs is a Hash with string keys
84:     def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
85:       if attrs.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
86:         attrs = attrs.to_unsafe_hash
87:       end
88: 
89:       return unless attrs.is_a?(Hash)
90:       self.attributes = delete_unsafe_attributes(attrs, user)
91:     end
92:   end
93: end

23:       # Returns the number of working days between from and to
24:       def working_days(from, to)
25:         days = (to - from).to_i
26:         if days > 0
27:           weeks = days / 7
28:           result = weeks * (7 - non_working_week_days.size)
29:           days_left = days - weeks * 7
30:           start_cwday = from.cwday
31:           days_left.times do |i|
32:             unless non_working_week_days.include?(((start_cwday + i - 1) % 7) + 1)
33:               result += 1
34:             end
35:           end
36:           result
37:         else
38:           0
39:         end
40:       end
41: 
42:       # Adds working days to the given date
43:       def add_working_days(date, working_days)
44:         if working_days > 0
45:           weeks = working_days / (7 - non_working_week_days.size)
46:           result = weeks * 7
47:           days_left = working_days - weeks * (7 - non_working_week_days.size)
48:           cwday = date.cwday
49:           while days_left > 0
50:             cwday += 1
51:             unless non_working_week_days.include?(((cwday - 1) % 7) + 1)
52:               days_left -= 1
53:             end
54:             result += 1
55:           end
56:           next_working_date(date + result)
57:         else
58:           date
59:         end
60:       end
61: 
62:       # Returns the date of the first day on or after the given date that is a working day
63:       def next_working_date(date)
64:         cwday = date.cwday
65:         days = 0
66:         days += 1 while non_working_week_days.include?(((cwday + days - 1) % 7) + 1)
67:         date + days
68:       end
69: 
70:       # Returns the index of non working week days (1=monday, 7=sunday)
71:       def non_working_week_days
72:         @non_working_week_days ||= begin
73:           days = Setting.non_working_week_days
74:           if days.is_a?(Array) && days.size < 7
75:             days.map(&:to_i)
76:           else
77:             []
78:           end
79:         end
80:       end
81:     end
82:   end
83: end

22: class Mailer < ActionMailer::Base
23:   layout 'mailer'

33: 
34:     around_enqueue :keep_current_user

37:   self.delivery_job = DeliveryJob

39:   # Overrides ActionMailer::Base#process in order to set the recipient as the current user
40:   # and his language as the default locale.
41:   # The first argument of all actions of this Mailer must be a User (the recipient),
42:   # otherwise an ArgumentError is raised.
43:   def process(action, *args)
44:     user = args.first
45:     raise ArgumentError, "First argument has to be a user, was #{user.inspect}" unless user.is_a?(User)
46: 
47:     initial_user = User.current
48:     initial_language = ::I18n.locale
49:     begin
50:       User.current = user
51: 
52:       lang = find_language(user.language) if user.logged?
53:       lang ||= Setting.default_language
54:       set_language_if_valid(lang)
55: 
56:       super
57:     ensure
58:       User.current = initial_user
59:       ::I18n.locale = initial_language
60:     end
61:   end
62: 
63:   # Default URL options for generating URLs in emails based on host_name and protocol
64:   # defined in application settings.
65:   def self.default_url_options
66:     options = {:protocol => Setting.protocol}
67:     if Setting.host_name.to_s =~ /\A(https?\:\/\/)?(.+?)(\:(\d+))?(\/.+)?\z/i
68:       host, port, prefix = $2, $4, $5
69:       options.merge!(
70:         {
71:           :host => host, :port => port, :script_name => prefix
72:         }
73:       )
74:     else
75:       options[:host] = Setting.host_name
76:     end
77:     options
78:   end
79: 
80:   # Builds a mail for notifying user about a new issue
81:   def issue_add(user, issue)
82:     redmine_headers 'Project' => issue.project.identifier,
83:                     'Issue-Tracker' => issue.tracker.name,
84:                     'Issue-Id' => issue.id,
85:                     'Issue-Author' => issue.author.login,
86:                     'Issue-Assignee' => assignee_for_header(issue)
87:     redmine_headers 'Issue-Priority' => issue.priority.name if issue.priority
88:     message_id issue
89:     references issue
90:     @author = issue.author
91:     @issue = issue
92:     @user = user
93:     @issue_url = url_for(:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => issue)
94:     subject = "[#{issue.project.name} - #{issue.tracker.name} ##{issue.id}]"
95:     subject += " (#{issue.status.name})" if Setting.show_status_changes_in_mail_subject?
96:     subject += " #{issue.subject}"
97:     mail :to => user,
98:       :subject => subject
99:   end

101:   # Notifies users about a new issue.
102:   #
103:   # Example:
104:   #   Mailer.deliver_issue_add(issue)
105:   def self.deliver_issue_add(issue)
106:     users = issue.notified_users | issue.notified_watchers | issue.notified_mentions
107:     users.each do |user|
108:       issue_add(user, issue).deliver_later
109:     end
110:   end

112:   # Builds a mail for notifying user about an issue update
113:   def issue_edit(user, journal)
114:     issue = journal.journalized
115:     redmine_headers 'Project' => issue.project.identifier,
116:                     'Issue-Tracker' => issue.tracker.name,
117:                     'Issue-Id' => issue.id,
118:                     'Issue-Author' => issue.author.login,
119:                     'Issue-Assignee' => assignee_for_header(issue)
120:     redmine_headers 'Issue-Priority' => issue.priority.name if issue.priority
121:     message_id journal
122:     references issue
123:     @author = journal.user
124:     s = "[#{issue.project.name} - #{issue.tracker.name} ##{issue.id}] "
125:     s += "(#{issue.status.name}) " if journal.new_value_for('status_id') && Setting.show_status_changes_in_mail_subject?
126:     s += issue.subject
127:     @issue = issue
128:     @user = user
129:     @journal = journal
130:     @journal_details = journal.visible_details
131:     @issue_url = url_for(:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => issue, :anchor => "change-#{journal.id}")
132: 
133:     mail :to => user,
134:       :subject => s
135:   end
136: 
137:   # Notifies users about an issue update.
138:   #
139:   # Example:
140:   #   Mailer.deliver_issue_edit(journal)
141:   def self.deliver_issue_edit(journal)
142:     users  = journal.notified_users | journal.notified_watchers | journal.notified_mentions | journal.journalized.notified_mentions
143:     users.select! do |user|
144:       journal.notes? || journal.visible_details(user).any?
145:     end
146:     users.each do |user|
147:       issue_edit(user, journal).deliver_later
148:     end
149:   end
150: 
151:   # Builds a mail to user about a new document.
152:   def document_added(user, document, author)
153:     redmine_headers 'Project' => document.project.identifier
154:     @author = author
155:     @document = document
156:     @user = user
157:     @document_url = url_for(:controller => 'documents', :action => 'show', :id => document)
158:     mail :to => user,
159:       :subject => "[#{document.project.name}] #{l(:label_document_new)}: #{document.title}"
160:   end
161: 
162:   # Notifies users that document was created by author
163:   #
164:   # Example:
165:   #   Mailer.deliver_document_added(document, author)
166:   def self.deliver_document_added(document, author)
167:     users = document.notified_users
168:     users.each do |user|
169:       document_added(user, document, author).deliver_later
170:     end
171:   end

173:   # Builds a mail to user about new attachements.
174:   def attachments_added(user, attachments)
175:     container = attachments.first.container
176:     added_to = ''
177:     added_to_url = ''
178:     @author = attachments.first.author
179:     case container.class.name
180:     when 'Project'
181:       added_to_url = url_for(:controller => 'files', :action => 'index', :project_id => container)
182:       added_to = "#{l(:label_project)}: #{container}"
183:     when 'Version'
184:       added_to_url = url_for(:controller => 'files', :action => 'index', :project_id => container.project)
185:       added_to = "#{l(:label_version)}: #{container.name}"
186:     when 'Document'
187:       added_to_url = url_for(:controller => 'documents', :action => 'show', :id => container.id)
188:       added_to = "#{l(:label_document)}: #{container.title}"
189:     end
190:     summary = l(:label_attachment_summary,
191:                 :filename => attachments.first.filename,
192:                 :count => attachments.length - 1)
193:     redmine_headers 'Project' => container.project.identifier
194:     @attachments = attachments
195:     @user = user
196:     @added_to = added_to
197:     @added_to_url = added_to_url
198:     mail :to => user,
199:          :subject => "[#{container.project.name}] #{l(:label_attachment_new)}: #{summary}"
200:   end

600:   # * :project  => id or identifier of project to process (defaults to all projects)
601:   # * :users    => array of user/group ids who should be reminded
602:   # * :version  => name of target version for filtering issues (defaults to none)
603:   def self.reminders(options={})
604:     days = options[:days] || 7
605:     project = options[:project] ? Project.find(options[:project]) : nil
606:     tracker = options[:tracker] ? Tracker.find(options[:tracker]) : nil
607:     target_version_id = options[:version] ? Version.named(options[:version]).pluck(:id) : nil
608:     if options[:version] && target_version_id.blank?
609:       raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound.new("Couldn't find Version named #{options[:version]}")
610:     end
611: 
612:     user_ids = options[:users]
613: 
614:     scope =
615:       Issue.open.where(
616:         "#{Issue.table_name}.assigned_to_id IS NOT NULL" \
617:           " AND #{Project.table_name}.status = #{Project::STATUS_ACTIVE}" \
618:           " AND #{Issue.table_name}.due_date <= ?", days.day.from_now.to_date
619:       )
620:     scope = scope.where(:assigned_to_id => user_ids) if user_ids.present?
621:     scope = scope.where(:project_id => project.id) if project
622:     scope = scope.where(:fixed_version_id => target_version_id) if target_version_id.present?
623:     scope = scope.where(:tracker_id => tracker.id) if tracker
624:     issues_by_assignee = scope.includes(:status, :assigned_to, :project, :tracker).
625:                               group_by(&:assigned_to)
626:     issues_by_assignee.keys.each do |assignee|  # rubocop:disable Style/HashEachMethods
627:       if assignee.is_a?(Group)
628:         assignee.users.each do |user|
629:           issues_by_assignee[user] ||= []
630:           issues_by_assignee[user] += issues_by_assignee[assignee]
631:         end
632:       end
633:     end
634: 
635:     issues_by_assignee.each do |assignee, issues|
636:       if assignee.is_a?(User) && assignee.active? && issues.present?
637:         visible_issues = issues.select {|i| i.visible?(assignee)}
638:         visible_issues.sort!{|a, b| (a.due_date <=> b.due_date).nonzero? || (a.id <=> b.id)}
639:         reminder(assignee, visible_issues, days).deliver_later if visible_issues.present?
640:       end
641:     end
642:   end
643: 
644:   # Activates/desactivates email deliveries during +block+
645:   def self.with_deliveries(enabled = true, &)
646:     was_enabled = ActionMailer::Base.perform_deliveries
647:     ActionMailer::Base.perform_deliveries = !!enabled
648:     yield
649:   ensure
650:     ActionMailer::Base.perform_deliveries = was_enabled
651:   end
652: 
653:   # Execute the given block with inline sending of emails if the default Async
654:   # queue is used for the mailer. See the Rails guide:
655:   # Using the asynchronous queue from a Rake task will generally not work because
656:   # Rake will likely end, causing the in-process thread pool to be deleted, before
657:   # any/all of the .deliver_later emails are processed
658:   def self.with_synched_deliveries(&)
659:     adapter = ActionMailer::MailDeliveryJob.queue_adapter
660:     ActionMailer::MailDeliveryJob.queue_adapter = ActiveJob::QueueAdapters::InlineAdapter.new
661:     yield
662:   ensure
663:     ActionMailer::MailDeliveryJob.queue_adapter = adapter
664:   end
665: 
666:   def mail(headers={}, &block)
667:     begin
668:       # Add a display name to the From field if Setting.mail_from does not
669:       # include it
670:       mail_from = Mail::Address.new(Setting.mail_from)
671:       if mail_from.display_name.blank? && mail_from.comments.blank?
672:         mail_from.display_name =
673:           @author&.logged? ? @author.name : Setting.app_title
674:       end
675:       from = mail_from.format
676: 
677:       # Construct the value of the List-Id header field
678:       from_addr = mail_from.address.to_s
679:       project_identifier = self.headers['X-Redmine-Project']&.value
680:       list_id = if project_identifier.present?
681:                   "<#{project_identifier}.#{from_addr.tr('@', '.')}>"
682:                 else
683:                   # Emails outside of a project context
684:                   "<#{from_addr.tr('@', '.')}>"
685:                 end
686:     rescue Mail::Field::IncompleteParseError
687:       # Use Setting.mail_from as it is if Mail::Address cannot parse it
688:       # (probably the emission address is not RFC compliant)
689:       from = Setting.mail_from.to_s
690:       list_id = "<#{from.tr('@', '.')}>"
691:     end
692: 
693:     headers.reverse_merge! 'X-Mailer' => 'Redmine',
694:             'X-Redmine-Host' => Setting.host_name,
695:             'X-Redmine-Site' => Setting.app_title,
696:             'X-Auto-Response-Suppress' => 'All',
697:             'Auto-Submitted' => 'auto-generated',
698:             'From' => from,
699:             'List-Id' => list_id
700: 
701:     # Replaces users with their email addresses
702:     [:to, :cc, :bcc].each do |key|
703:       if headers[key].present?
704:         headers[key] = self.class.email_addresses(headers[key])
705:       end
706:     end
707: 
708:     # Removes the author from the recipients and cc
709:     # if the author does not want to receive notifications
710:     # about what the author do
711:     if @author&.logged? && @author.pref.no_self_notified
712:       addresses = @author.mails
713:       headers[:to] -= addresses if headers[:to].is_a?(Array)
714:       headers[:cc] -= addresses if headers[:cc].is_a?(Array)
715:     end
716: 
717:     if @author&.logged?
718:       redmine_headers 'Sender' => @author.login
719:     end
720: 
721:     if @message_id_object
722:       headers[:message_id] = "<#{self.class.message_id_for(@message_id_object, @user)}>"
723:     end
724:     if @references_objects
725:       headers[:references] = @references_objects.collect {|o| "<#{self.class.references_for(o, @user)}>"}.join(' ')
726:     end
727: 
728:     if block
729:       super
730:     else
731:       super do |format|
732:         format.text
733:         format.html unless Setting.plain_text_mail?
734:       end
735:     end
736:   end
737: 
738:   def self.deliver_mail(mail)
739:     return false if mail.to.blank? && mail.cc.blank? && mail.bcc.blank?
740: 
741:     begin
742:       # Log errors when raise_delivery_errors is set to false, Rails does not
743:       mail.raise_delivery_errors = true
744:       super
745:     rescue => e
746:       if ActionMailer::Base.raise_delivery_errors
747:         raise e
748:       else
749:         Rails.logger.error "Email delivery error: #{e.message}"

20: class Webhook < ApplicationRecord
21:   Executor = Struct.new(:url, :payload, :secret) do
22:     # @return [Net::HTTP::Response] if the POST request was successful
23:     # @raise [Exception] one of a lot of possible exceptions if the webhook was
24:     #   not successfully send, e.g. if we could not find valid IPs or could not
25:     #   connect to any of them or if an unexpected (i.e. non-successful)
26:     #   response status was set; it may contain the server response.
27:     def call
28:       headers = { accept: '*/*', 'content-type': 'application/json', 'user-agent': 'Redmine' }
29:       if secret.present?
30:         headers['X-Redmine-Signature-256'] = compute_signature
31:       end
32:       Rails.logger.debug { "Webhook: POST #{url}" }
33: 
34:       url = URI.parse(self.url)
35:       valid_ips.each do |ip|
36:         begin
37:           Rails.logger.debug { "Trying #{ip}" }
38:           http = ::Net::HTTP.start(
39:             url.host,
40:             url.port,
41:             open_timeout: 60,
42:             read_timeout: 60,
43:             write_timeout: 60,
44:             use_ssl: (url.scheme == 'https'),
45:             ipaddr: ip
46:           )
47:         rescue
48:           # We could not create a HTTP(S) connection to the IP.
49:           begin
50:             http&.finish
51:           rescue
52:             nil
53:           end
54: 
55:           # Since we have not sent any actual request data, we continue with the
56:           # next IP (if any).
57:           next
58:         end
59: 
60:         request = ::Net::HTTP::Post.new(url, headers)
61:         request.basic_auth url.user, url.password if url.user
62:         request.body = payload
63: 
64:         # Return the response or raise if there was any transport error,
65:         # timeout, or non-successful HTTP response code
66:         return http.request(request).tap(&:value)
67:       ensure
68:         begin
69:           http&.finish
70:         rescue
71:           nil
72:         end
73:       end
74: 
75:       raise SocketError, "Could not connect to any IP"
76:     end
77: 
78:     # Returns a list of IP addresses for the hostname of the URI, or an empty
79:     # array if no IPs were found or any of them were invalid
80:     def valid_ips
81:       WebhookEndpointValidator.ips_for_uri(url)
82:     end
83: 
84:     # Computes the HMAC signature for the given payload and secret.
85:     # https://docs.github.com/en/webhooks/using-webhooks/validating-webhook-deliveries
86:     def compute_signature
87:       'sha256=' + OpenSSL::HMAC.hexdigest(OpenSSL::Digest.new('sha256'), secret, payload)
88:     end
89:   end
90: 
91:   belongs_to :user
92:   has_and_belongs_to_many :projects # rubocop:disable Rails/HasAndBelongsToMany
93: 
94:   validates :url, presence: true, webhook_endpoint: true, length: { maximum: 2000 }
95:   validates :secret, length: { maximum: 255 }, allow_blank: true
96:   validate :check_events_array
97: 
98:   serialize :events, coder: YAML, type: Array
99: 
100:   scope :active, -> { where(active: true) }
101: 
102:   before_validation ->(hook){ hook.projects = hook.projects.to_a & hook.setable_projects }
103: 
104:   def self.enabled?
105:     Setting.webhooks_enabled?
106:   end
107: 
108:   # Triggers the given event for the given object, scheduling qualifying hooks
109:   # to be called.
110:   def self.trigger(event, object)
111:     return unless enabled?
112: 
113:     hooks_for(event, object).each do |hook|
114:       payload = hook.payload(event, object)
115:       WebhookJob.perform_later(hook.id, payload.to_json)
116:     end
117:   end
118: 
119:   # Finds hooks for the given event and object.
120:   # Returns an array of hooks that are active, have the given event in their list
121:   # of events, and whose user can see the object.
122:   #
123:   # Object must have a project_id and respond to visible?(user)
124:   def self.hooks_for(event, object)
125:     Webhook.active
126:       .joins("INNER JOIN projects_webhooks on projects_webhooks.webhook_id = webhooks.id")
127:       .eager_load(:user)
128:       .where(users: { status: User::STATUS_ACTIVE }, projects_webhooks: { project_id: object.project_id })
129:       .to_a.select do |hook|
130:       hook.events.include?(event) && object.visible?(hook.user) && hook.user.allowed_to?(:use_webhooks, object.project)
131:     end
132:   end
133: 
134:   def setable_projects
135:     user = self.user || User.current
136:     Project.visible(user).to_a.select{|p| user.allowed_to?(:use_webhooks, p)}
137:   end
138: 
139:   def setable_events
140:     WebhookPayload.events
141:   end
142: 
143:   def setable_event_names
144:     setable_events.map{|type, actions| actions.map{|action| "#{type}.#{action}"}}.flatten
145:   end
146: 
147:   # computes the payload. this happens when the hook is triggered, and the
148:   # payload is stored as part of the hook job definition.
149:   # event must be of the form 'type.action' (like 'issue.created')
150:   def payload(event, object)
151:     WebhookPayload.new(event, object, user).to_h
152:   end
153: 
154:   # POSTs the given payload to the hook URL, returns true if successful, false otherwise.
155:   #
156:   # logs any unsuccessful hook calls, but does not raise
157:   def call(payload_json)
158:     Executor.new(url, payload_json, secret).call
159:     true
160:   rescue => e
161:     Rails.logger.warn { "Webhook Error: #{e.message} (#{e.class})\n#{e.backtrace.join "\n"}" }
162:     false
163:   end
164: 
165:   private
166: 
167:   def check_events_array
168:     unless events.is_a?(Array)
169:       errors.add(:events, :invalid)
170:       return
171:     end
172: 
173:     events.reject!(&:blank?)
174:     if (events - setable_event_names).any?
175:       errors.add(:events, :invalid)
176:     end
177:   end
178: end

20: # Webhook payload
21: class WebhookPayload
22:   attr_accessor :event, :object, :user
23: 
24:   def initialize(event, object, user)
25:     self.event = event
26:     self.object = object
27:     self.user = user
28:   end
29: 
30:   def self.register_model(model, model_events)
31:     raise ArgumentError, "model_events must be Array" unless model_events.is_a?(Array)
32: 
33:     @events ||= {}
34:     @events[model.model_name.singular.to_sym] = model_events
35:   end
36: 
37:   def self.events
38:     @events ||= {}
39:   end
40: 
41:   def to_h
42:     type, action = event.split('.')
43:     if self.class.events[type.to_sym]&.include?(action)
44:       object.webhook_payload(user, action)
45:     else
46:       raise ArgumentError, "invalid event: #{event}"
47:     end
48:   end

50:   # given a path to an API template (relative to RAILS_ROOT), renders it and returns the resulting hash
51:   class ApiRenderer
52:     include ApplicationHelper
53:     include CustomFieldsHelper
54:     attr_accessor :path, :params, :user
55: 
56:     DummyRequest = Struct.new(:params)
57: 
58:     def initialize(path, user, params = nil)
59:       self.path = path
60:       self.user = user
61:       self.params = params || {}
62:     end
63: 
64:     def to_h(**ivars)
65:       req = DummyRequest.new(params)
66:       api = Redmine::Views::Builders::Json.new(req, nil)
67:       ivars.each { |k, v| instance_variable_set :"@#{k}", v }
68:       original_user = User.current
69:       begin
70:         User.current = self.user
71:         instance_eval(File.read(Rails.root.join(path)), path, 1)
72:       ensure
73:         User.current = original_user
74:       end
75:     end
76:   end
77: end

20: class IssueImport < Import
21:   AUTO_MAPPABLE_FIELDS = {
22:     'tracker' => 'field_tracker',
23:     'subject' => 'field_subject',
24:     'description' => 'field_description',
25:     'status' => 'field_status',
26:     'priority' => 'field_priority',
27:     'category' => 'field_category',
28:     'assigned_to' => 'field_assigned_to',
29:     'fixed_version' => 'field_fixed_version',
30:     'is_private' => 'field_is_private',
31:     'parent_issue_id' => 'field_parent_issue',
32:     'start_date' => 'field_start_date',
33:     'due_date' => 'field_due_date',
34:     'estimated_hours' => 'field_estimated_hours',
35:     'done_ratio' => 'field_done_ratio',
36:     'unique_id' => 'field_unique_id',
37:     'relation_duplicates' => 'label_duplicates',
38:     'relation_duplicated' => 'label_duplicated_by',
39:     'relation_blocks' => 'label_blocks',
40:     'relation_blocked' => 'label_blocked_by',
41:     'relation_relates' => 'label_relates_to',
42:     'relation_precedes' => 'label_precedes',
43:     'relation_follows' =>  'label_follows',
44:     'relation_copied_to' => 'label_copied_to',
45:     'relation_copied_from' => 'label_copied_from'
46:   }
47: 
48:   def self.menu_item
49:     :issues
50:   end
51: 
52:   def self.authorized?(user)
53:     user.allowed_to?(:import_issues, nil, :global => true) && user.allowed_to?(:add_issues, nil, :global => true)
54:   end
55: 
56:   # Returns the objects that were imported
57:   def saved_objects
58:     object_ids = saved_items.pluck(:obj_id)
59:     objects = Issue.where(:id => object_ids).order(:id).preload(:tracker, :priority, :status)
60:   end
61: 
62:   # Returns a scope of projects that user is allowed to
63:   # import issue to
64:   def allowed_target_projects
65:     Project.allowed_to(user, :import_issues)
66:   end
67: 
68:   def project
69:     project_id = mapping['project_id'].to_i
70:     allowed_target_projects.find_by_id(project_id) || allowed_target_projects.first
71:   end
72: 
73:   # Returns a scope of trackers that user is allowed to
74:   # import issue to
75:   def allowed_target_trackers
76:     Issue.allowed_target_trackers(project, user)
77:   end
78: 
79:   def tracker
80:     if mapping['tracker'].to_s =~ /\Avalue:(\d+)\z/
81:       tracker_id = $1.to_i
82:       allowed_target_trackers.find_by_id(tracker_id)
83:     end
84:   end
85: 
86:   # Returns true if missing categories should be created during the import
87:   def create_categories?
88:     user.allowed_to?(:manage_categories, project) &&
89:       mapping['create_categories'] == '1'
90:   end
91: 
92:   # Returns true if missing versions should be created during the import
93:   def create_versions?
94:     user.allowed_to?(:manage_versions, project) &&
95:       mapping['create_versions'] == '1'
96:   end
97: 
98:   def mappable_custom_fields
99:     if tracker
100:       issue = Issue.new
101:       issue.project = project
102:       issue.tracker = tracker
103:       issue.editable_custom_field_values(user).map(&:custom_field)
104:     elsif project
105:       project.all_issue_custom_fields
106:     else
107:       []
108:     end
109:   end
110: 
111:   private
112: 
113:   def build_object(row, item)
114:     issue = Issue.new
115:     issue.author = user
116:     issue.notify = !!ActiveRecord::Type::Boolean.new.cast(settings['notifications'])
117: 
118:     tracker_id = nil
119:     if tracker
120:       tracker_id = tracker.id
121:     elsif tracker_name = row_value(row, 'tracker')
122:       tracker_id = allowed_target_trackers.named(tracker_name).first.try(:id)
123:     end
124: 
125:     attributes = {
126:       'project_id' => mapping['project_id'],
127:       'tracker_id' => tracker_id,
128:       'subject' => row_value(row, 'subject'),
129:       'description' => row_value(row, 'description')
130:     }
131:     if status_name = row_value(row, 'status')
132:       if status_id = IssueStatus.named(status_name).first.try(:id)
133:         attributes['status_id'] = status_id
134:       end
135:     end
136:     issue.send :safe_attributes=, attributes, user
137: 
138:     attributes = {}
139:     if priority_name = row_value(row, 'priority')
140:       if priority_id = IssuePriority.active.named(priority_name).first.try(:id)
141:         attributes['priority_id'] = priority_id
142:       end
143:     end
144:     if issue.project && category_name = row_value(row, 'category')
145:       if category = issue.project.issue_categories.named(category_name).first
146:         attributes['category_id'] = category.id
147:       elsif create_categories?
148:         category = issue.project.issue_categories.build
149:         category.name = category_name
150:         if category.save
151:           attributes['category_id'] = category.id
152:         end
153:       end
154:     end
155:     if assignee_name = row_value(row, 'assigned_to')
156:       if assignee = Principal.detect_by_keyword(issue.assignable_users, assignee_name)
157:         attributes['assigned_to_id'] = assignee.id
158:       end
159:     end
160:     if issue.project && version_name = row_value(row, 'fixed_version')
161:       version =
162:         issue.project.versions.named(version_name).first ||
163:         issue.project.shared_versions.named(version_name).first
164:       if version
165:         attributes['fixed_version_id'] = version.id
166:       elsif create_versions?
167:         version = issue.project.versions.build
168:         version.name = version_name
169:         if version.save
170:           attributes['fixed_version_id'] = version.id
171:         end
172:       end
173:     end
174:     if is_private = row_value(row, 'is_private')
175:       if yes?(is_private)
176:         attributes['is_private'] = '1'
177:       end
178:     end
179:     if parent_issue_id = row_value(row, 'parent_issue_id')
180:       if parent_issue_id.start_with? '#'
181:         # refers to existing issue
182:         attributes['parent_issue_id'] = parent_issue_id[1..-1]
183:       elsif use_unique_id?
184:         # refers to other row with unique id
185:         issue_id = items.where(:unique_id => parent_issue_id).first.try(:obj_id)
186: 
187:         if issue_id
188:           attributes['parent_issue_id'] = issue_id
189:         else
190:           add_callback(parent_issue_id, 'set_as_parent', item.position)
191:         end
192:       elsif /\A\d+\z/.match?(parent_issue_id)
193:         # refers to other row by position
194:         parent_issue_id = parent_issue_id.to_i
195: 
196:         if parent_issue_id > item.position
197:           add_callback(parent_issue_id, 'set_as_parent', item.position)
198:         elsif issue_id = items.where(:position => parent_issue_id).first.try(:obj_id)
199:           attributes['parent_issue_id'] = issue_id
200:         end
201: 
202:       else
203:         # Something is odd. Assign parent_issue_id to trigger validation error
204:         attributes['parent_issue_id'] = parent_issue_id
205:       end
206:     end
207:     if start_date = row_date(row, 'start_date')
208:       attributes['start_date'] = start_date
209:     end
210:     if due_date = row_date(row, 'due_date')
211:       attributes['due_date'] = due_date
212:     end
213:     if estimated_hours = row_value(row, 'estimated_hours')
214:       attributes['estimated_hours'] = estimated_hours
215:     end
216:     if done_ratio = row_value(row, 'done_ratio')
217:       attributes['done_ratio'] = done_ratio
218:     end
219: 
220:     attributes['custom_field_values'] = issue.custom_field_values.inject({}) do |h, v|
221:       value =
222:         case v.custom_field.field_format
223:         when 'date'
224:           row_date(row, "cf_#{v.custom_field.id}")
225:         else
226:           row_value(row, "cf_#{v.custom_field.id}")
227:         end
228:       if value
229:         h[v.custom_field.id.to_s] = v.custom_field.value_from_keyword(value, issue)
230:       end
231:       h
232:     end
233: 
234:     issue.send :safe_attributes=, attributes, user
235: 
236:     if issue.tracker_id != tracker_id
237:       issue.tracker_id = nil
238:     end
239: 
240:     issue
241:   end
242: 
243:   def extend_object(row, item, issue)
244:     build_relations(row, item, issue)
245:   end
246: 
247:   def build_relations(row, item, issue)
248:     IssueRelation::TYPES.each_key do |type|
249:       has_delay = [IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES, IssueRelation::TYPE_FOLLOWS].include?(type)
250: 
251:       if decls = relation_values(row, "relation_#{type}")
252:         decls.each do |decl|
253:           unless decl[:matches]
254:             # Invalid relation syntax - doesn't match regexp
255:             next
256:           end
257: 
258:           if decl[:delay] && !has_delay
259:             # Invalid relation syntax - delay for relation that doesn't support delays
260:             next
261:           end
262: 
263:           relation = IssueRelation.new(
264:             "relation_type" => type,
265:             "issue_from_id" => issue.id
266:           )
267: 
268:           if decl[:other_id]
269:             relation.issue_to_id = decl[:other_id]
270:           elsif decl[:other_pos]
271:             if use_unique_id?
272:               issue_id = items.where(:unique_id => decl[:other_pos]).first.try(:obj_id)
273:               if issue_id
274:                 relation.issue_to_id = issue_id
275:               else
276:                 add_callback(decl[:other_pos], 'set_relation', item.position, type, decl[:delay])
277:                 next
278:               end
279:             elsif decl[:other_pos] > item.position
280:               add_callback(decl[:other_pos], 'set_relation', item.position, type, decl[:delay])
281:               next
282:             elsif issue_id = items.where(:position => decl[:other_pos]).first.try(:obj_id)
283:               relation.issue_to_id = issue_id
284:             end
285:           end
286: 
287:           relation.delay = decl[:delay] if decl[:delay]
288: 
289:           begin
290:             relation.save!
291:           rescue
292:             nil
293:           end
294:         end
295:       end
296:     end
297: 
298:     issue
299:   end
300: 
301:   def relation_values(row, name)
302:     content = row_value(row, name)
303: 
304:     return if content.blank?
305: 
306:     content.split(",").map do |declaration|
307:       declaration = declaration.strip
308: 
309:       # Valid expression:
310:       #
311:       # 123  => row 123 within the CSV
312:       # #123 => issue with ID 123
313:       #
314:       # For precedes and follows
315:       #
316:       # 123 7d    => row 123 within CSV with 7 day delay
317:       # #123  7d  => issue with ID 123 with 7 day delay
318:       # 123 -3d   => negative delay allowed
319:       #
320:       #
321:       # Invalid expression:
322:       #
323:       # No. 123 => Invalid leading letters
324:       # # 123   => Invalid space between # and issue number
325:       # 123 8h  => No other time units allowed (just days)
326:       #
327:       # Please note: If unique_id mapping is present, the whole line - but the
328:       # trailing delay expression - is considered unique_id.
329:       #
330:       # See examples at Rubular http://rubular.com/r/mgXM5Rp6zK
331:       #
332:       match = declaration.match(/\A(?<unique_id>(?<is_id>#)?(?<id>\d+)|.+?)(?:\s+(?<delay>-?\d+)d)?\z/)
333: 
334:       result = {
335:         :matches     => false,
336:         :declaration => declaration
337:       }
338: 
339:       if match
340:         result[:matches] = true
341:         result[:delay]   = match[:delay]
342: 
343:         if match[:is_id] && match[:id]
344:           result[:other_id] = match[:id]
345:         elsif use_unique_id? && match[:unique_id]
346:           result[:other_pos] = match[:unique_id]
347:         elsif match[:id]
348:           result[:other_pos] = match[:id].to_i
349:         else
350:           result[:matches] = false
351:         end
352:       end
353: 
354:       result
355:     end
356:   end
357: 
358:   # Callback that sets issue as the parent of a previously imported issue
359:   def set_as_parent_callback(issue, child_position)
360:     child_id = items.where(:position => child_position).first.try(:obj_id)
361:     return unless child_id
362: 
363:     child = Issue.find_by_id(child_id)
364:     return unless child
365: 
366:     child.parent_issue_id = issue.id
367:     child.save!
368:     issue.reload
369:   end
370: 
371:   def set_relation_callback(to_issue, from_position, type, delay)
372:     return if to_issue.new_record?
373: 
374:     from_id = items.where(:position => from_position).first.try(:obj_id)
375:     return unless from_id
376: 
377:     IssueRelation.create!(
378:       'relation_type' => type,
379:       'issue_from_id' => from_id,
380:       'issue_to_id'   => to_issue.id,
381:       'delay'         => delay
382:     )
383:     to_issue.reload
384:   end
385: end

(End of file - total 385 lines)

20: class CalendarsController < ApplicationController
21:   menu_item :calendar
22:   before_action :find_optional_project
23: 
24:   rescue_from Query::StatementInvalid, :with => :query_statement_invalid
25: 
26:   helper :issues
27:   helper :projects
28:   helper :queries
29:   include QueriesHelper
30: 
31:   def show
32:     if params[:year] and params[:year].to_i > 1900
33:       @year = params[:year].to_i
34:       if params[:month] and params[:month].to_i > 0 and params[:month].to_i < 13
35:         @month = params[:month].to_i
36:       end
37:     end
38:     @year ||= User.current.today.year
39:     @month ||= User.current.today.month
40: 
41:     @calendar = Redmine::Helpers::Calendar.new(Date.civil(@year, @month, 1), current_language, :month)
42:     retrieve_query
43:     @query.group_by = nil
44:     @query.sort_criteria = nil
45:     if @query.valid?
46:       events = []
47:       events +=
48:         @query.issues(
49:           :include => [:tracker, :assigned_to, :priority],
50:           :conditions => [
51:             "((start_date BETWEEN ? AND ?) OR (due_date BETWEEN ? AND ?))",
52:             @calendar.startdt, @calendar.enddt,
53:             @calendar.startdt, @calendar.enddt
54:           ]
55:         )
56:       events +=
57:         @query.versions(
58:           :conditions => [
59:             "effective_date BETWEEN ? AND ?",
60:             @calendar.startdt, @calendar.enddt
61:           ]
62:         )
63:       @calendar.events = events
64:     end
65: 
66:     render :action => 'show', :layout => false if request.xhr?
67:   end
68: end
